content: We can model dynamic prefrontal-hippocampal memory retrieval interactions as a query-adaptive graph index for embodied AI active learning agents, where the prefrontal cortex acts as a graph index routing mechanism that prunes irrelevant hippocampal memory nodes to stay within the agent’s working memory capacity limits during sequential reasoning. This brain-inspired graph indexing design automatically prioritizes retrieval of context-relevant memory traces to avoid capacity overflow while improving retrieval speed for downstream active learning tasks.
